description: Document Artifact Re-Processing Workflow - Multi-pass review pipeline with fact-checking, voice/clarity editing, editorial review, and version history

userStories.title: Add ArtifactVersion model to Prisma schema
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to store previous versions of artifacts so that re-processing preserves the original content.
acceptanceCriteria: Create ArtifactVersion model in prisma/schema.prisma with fields: id (Int, autoincrement), documentArtifactId (Int, FK to DocumentArtifact), content (String), version (Int), createdAt (DateTime, default now())
ArtifactVersion has a many-to-one relation to DocumentArtifact (field: documentArtifact)
Add versions DocumentArtifact[] relation field to DocumentArtifact model
Generate and run migration: npx prisma migrate dev --name add_artifact_version
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: File: prisma/schema.prisma. Follow existing model patterns. After migration, run npx prisma generate to update the client.

userStories.title: Create saveArtifactVersion utility
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a utility that snapshots artifact content into version history before overwriting.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/utils/artifact-version.ts
Export async function saveArtifactVersion(prisma: PrismaClient, documentArtifactId: number): Promise<void>
Function reads current artifact content from DocumentArtifact by id
Determines next version number by counting existing ArtifactVersion records for that artifact + 1
Creates new ArtifactVersion record with the current content and computed version number
If artifact has no content or doesn't exist, skip without error
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use app.prisma pattern consistent with rest of codebase. Import PrismaClient type from @prisma/client.

userStories.title: Create reprocess feedback types and directory structure
userStories.description: As a developer, I need shared types for the reprocess pipeline feedback so all passes use consistent data structures.
acceptanceCriteria: Create directory src/routes/workflows/reprocess/
Create src/routes/workflows/reprocess/types.ts with exported types
Export FactCheckIssue type: { claim: string, severity: 'error' | 'warning', finding: string, suggestedCorrection: string }
Export FactCheckFeedback type: { artifactTitle: string, issues: FactCheckIssue[] }
Export VoiceClarityEdit type: { location: string, suggestion: string, rationale: string }
Export VoiceClarityFeedback type: { artifactTitle: string, edits: VoiceClarityEdit[] }
Export EditorialChange type: { type: 'completeness' | 'flow' | 'redundancy' | 'context' | 'coherence', description: string, recommendation: string }
Export EditorialFeedback type: { artifactTitle: string, overallAssessment: string, changes: EditorialChange[] }
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: These types are used as in-memory objects passed between pipeline passes. They are not persisted to DB.

userStories.title: Implement fact-check review pass
userStories.description: As a user, I want each artifact fact-checked against the original document and external sources so that inaccuracies are identified.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/routes/workflows/reprocess/fact-check.ts
Export async function factCheckArtifact(app: FastifyInstance, originalDocContent: string, artifactTitle: string, artifactContent: string): Promise<FactCheckFeedback>
Use planning-agent or reasoning model to compare artifact claims against originalDocContent
Use research tools (DuckDuckGo, Tavily, Wikipedia) via research agent for external verification of specific factual claims (dates, names, numbers, legal references)
Return structured FactCheckFeedback with list of issues found, each with severity and suggested correction
If no issues found, return empty issues array
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use existing research agent tools from src/tools/search.ts. Use reasoning model from ai-models.ts for the analysis LLM call. Follow existing workflow handler patterns for LLM invocation.

userStories.title: Implement voice and clarity edit pass
userStories.description: As a user, I want each artifact edited for voice and clarity so the content reads well and maintains consistent tone.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/routes/workflows/reprocess/voice-clarity.ts
Export async function voiceClarityReview(app: FastifyInstance, artifactTitle: string, artifactContent: string, factCheckFeedback: FactCheckFeedback): Promise<VoiceClarityFeedback>
Use reasoning model to review artifact for: readability, tone consistency, jargon usage, sentence structure, conciseness
Accept fact-check feedback as context so edits don't reintroduce factual issues
Return structured VoiceClarityFeedback with list of suggested edits and rationale
If no edits needed, return empty edits array
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use reasoning model from ai-models.ts. Prompt should instruct the LLM to return structured JSON matching the VoiceClarityFeedback type.

userStories.title: Implement editorial review pass
userStories.description: As a user, I want an editorial review of each artifact for overall quality, coherence, and completeness.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/routes/workflows/reprocess/editorial-review.ts

Export async function editorialReview(app: FastifyInstance, artifactTitle: string, artifactContent: string, factCheckFeedback: FactCheckFeedback, voiceClarityFeedback: VoiceClarityFeedback): Promise<EditorialFeedback>
Use reasoning model to review artifact for: completeness, logical flow, redundancy, missing context, alignment with document purpose
Accept both prior feedback objects as input context
Return structured EditorialFeedback with overall assessment and list of recommended changes
If no changes needed, return empty changes array with positive assessment
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use reasoning model from ai-models.ts. This is the final review pass before rewrite, so it should consider all prior feedback.

userStories.title: Implement final rewrite pass
userStories.description: As a user, I want a final rewrite that incorporates all review feedback to produce improved artifacts.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/routes/workflows/reprocess/final-rewrite.ts

Export async function finalRewrite(app: FastifyInstance, documentId: number, artifactTitle: string, artifactContent: string, factCheck: FactCheckFeedback, voiceClarity: VoiceClarityFeedback, editorial: EditorialFeedback): Promise<string>
Use pro model (or reasoning model) to rewrite artifact incorporating all three feedback sets
Call saveArtifactVersion(app.prisma, artifactId) before overwriting
Overwrite existing artifact content via prisma documentArtifact.update
Return the new content string
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use saveArtifactVersion from src/utils/artifact-version.ts (US-002). Fetch artifact ID via prisma findFirst with documentId + title compound key. Use pro model from ai-models.ts for highest quality rewrite.

userStories.title: Create reprocess orchestrator for single document
userStories.description: As a user, I want to trigger re-processing for a single document so all its artifacts are reviewed and improved.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/routes/workflows/reprocess/run-reprocess.ts
Export async function handleReprocessWorkflow(app: FastifyInstance, documentId: number)
Fetch all artifacts for document excluding 'Original Document' title
Fetch 'Original Document' artifact content for use as reference
Run pipeline sequentially: for each artifact run fact-check, then voice/clarity, then editorial review, then final rewrite
Within each pass, artifacts can be processed in parallel via Promise.all
Emit progress events: reprocess:pipeline-started, reprocess:step-started, reprocess:step-completed, reprocess:step-failed, reprocess:pipeline-completed via app.workflowEvents
Return summary object with per-artifact results (success/failure)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Follow pattern from run-all.ts orchestrator. Add reprocess event types to WorkflowEvents in document.ts. The pipeline is: fact-check all → voice/clarity all → editorial all → rewrite all.

userStories.title: Register reprocess API route
userStories.description: As a user, I want an API endpoint to trigger single-document re-processing.
acceptanceCriteria: Register GET /document/reprocess route with required query param documentId (number)
Route calls handleReprocessWorkflow(app, documentId) and returns the result
Add reprocess event types to WorkflowEvents type in document.ts: reprocess:pipeline-started, reprocess:step-started, reprocess:step-completed, reprocess:step-failed, reprocess:pipeline-completed
SSE endpoint (workflow-progress.ts) also forwards reprocess:* events to connected clients
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Register in document.ts alongside existing routes. Update WorkflowEvents type with new reprocess event signatures. Update workflow-progress.ts to listen for reprocess:* events.

userStories.title: Add batch re-processing API endpoint
userStories.description: As a user, I want to re-process a range of documents in one call.
acceptanceCriteria: Register GET /document/reprocess-batch route with required query params startId (number) and endId (number)
Query documents in range, process sequentially (one at a time) to manage LLM rate limits
Skip documents that don't exist or have no artifacts
Emit reprocess:batch-progress event with { currentDocumentId, completedCount, totalCount }
Return summary with per-document results: success, failure, or skipped
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Add reprocess:batch-progress event type to WorkflowEvents. Sequential processing avoids LLM concurrency limits. Use a for loop over document IDs, calling handleReprocessWorkflow for each.

userStories.title: Add version history API endpoint
userStories.description: As a user, I want to view the version history of an artifact.
acceptanceCriteria: Register GET /document/artifact-versions route with required query params documentId (number) and title (string)
Fetch DocumentArtifact by compound key (documentId, title)
Fetch all ArtifactVersion records for that artifact, ordered by version descending
Return array with current (live) content as latest entry plus all historical versions with version number and createdAt
Return 404 if artifact not found
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Register in document.ts. Use prisma documentArtifact.findUnique with documentId_title compound key, then prisma artifactVersion.findMany ordered by version desc.

userStories.title: Add dashboard re-processing controls
userStories.description: As a user, I want to trigger re-processing from the dashboard UI.
acceptanceCriteria: Add 'Re-Process' button to dashboard for the current document (below existing workflow cards)
Add 'Batch Re-Process' section with start ID and end ID number inputs and a 'Start Batch' button
Buttons call fetch() to respective API endpoints (GET /document/reprocess and /document/reprocess-batch)
Dashboard shows re-processing progress via SSE reprocess:* events (reuse existing card/progress UI pattern)
Disable buttons while re-processing is in progress
Show completion summary text when re-processing finishes
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Modify src/routes/dashboard.ts. Add new HTML section below existing workflow grid. Add EventSource listeners for reprocess:* events. Follow existing dashboard JS patterns for DOM updates.
